title: CSS Grid vs Flexbox Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on the differences and use cases for the two most popular CSS layout tools.

questions.question: Which of these is a one-dimensional layout system?
When should you use CSS Grid over Flexbox?
options: When aligning items in a single row.
When the layout is dictates by the content within it.
When you need precise control over both rows and columns simultaneously.
When you only need to center a single item.

questions.question: What is the primary difference in approach between Grid and Flexbox?
options: Grid is layout-first; Flexbox is content-first.
Grid is content-first; Flexbox is layout-first.
Grid only supports pixels; Flexbox only supports percentages.
Grid is for desktop; Flexbox is for mobile.

questions.question: True or False: Using both Grid and Flexbox on the same page is considered bad practice.
Which property would you use to define the columns in a CSS Grid?
